title: कृष्ण चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ दोहा ॥
बंसी शोभित कर मधुर, नीलमणि तन श्याम।
चंद्रिका सी मंद हँसी, नन्दकिशोर ललाम॥
अर्जुन को गीता कही, देवकी के बलराम।
कृष्ण चालीसा पढ़त ही, पूरन होत सब काम॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय श्रीकृष्ण दयानिधि दाता।
जग के पालनहार बिधाता॥
देवकी तुम मैया के ऊधव।
यशोदा के हित की परछाईं॥
नन्द के आनन्द बढ़ावन।
गोकुल में आनन्द मचावन॥
ब्रज में धूम मचावन लीला।
सुर-नर-मुनि मन हरने खेला॥
मैया के संग माखन भावै।
चोरी कर नवनीत उड़ावै॥
ग्वालन संग गैय्या चरावै।
बंसी की धुन सब मन भावै॥
गोपियन के संग रास रचावै।
मुरली की मधुर धुन गावै॥
कालिंदी में कालिय नाथा।
छापे विषधर लिये साथा॥
गोवर्धन गिरि को उठाया।
इंद्र का मान खूब घटाया॥
मथुरा में कंस वध कीना।
उग्रसेन को राज पद दीना॥
गुरु संदीपन से विद्या पाई।
मित्र सुदामा की सुधि आई॥
द्वारिका नगरी बसाई।
रुक्मिणी संग विवाह रचाई॥
पांडवन के सखा कहाये।
अर्जुन के सारथी बन आये॥
विश्वरूप अर्जुन दिखाया।
गीता ज्ञान जगत को दाया॥
धर्म की सदा रक्षा कीनी।
दुष्टन को सब सजा दीनी॥
द्रौपदी की लाज बचाई।
अन्त विहीन चीर बढ़ाई॥
भीष्म प्रण की राखी लाजा।
धर्मराज को दीन्हो राजा॥
सोलह कला सम्पूर्ण अवतारा।
कर्म-धर्म का दीप उजारा॥
जरासन्ध को दीन्हो मारी।
शिशुपाल वध की तैयारी॥
नरकासुर का नाश कराया।
सोलह सहस कन्या को लाया॥
गोपी गोपन को सुख दीना।
भक्तन पर सदा कृपा कीना॥
मीरा थी दीवानी तोरी।
सूरदास की आँख न मोरी॥
नरसी का कष्ट निवारा।
भक्तन को सदा प्यारा॥
मोर मुकुट पीतांबर धारी।
कुण्डल श्रवण मनोहर भारी॥
वैजयन्ती माल विराजे।
नयनन में अंजन साजे॥
मुरली अधर मधुर मुस्कान।
सकल सृष्टि के तुम भगवान॥
कृष्ण चालीसा जो गावै।
बैकुण्ठ परमपद पावै॥
जो कोई शुद्ध भाव से पढ़ई।
तन-मन-धन की विपदा कटई॥

॥ दोहा ॥
श्रीकृष्ण चालीसा नित, पढ़ै जो कोई धाम।
ताको सब सुख सम्पदा, मिलै कृष्ण अविराम॥
transliteration:
|| Doha ||
Bansi Shobhit Kar Madhur, Nilmani Tan Shyam.
Chandrika Si Mand Hansi, Nandkishor Lalam.
Arjun Ko Geeta Kahi, Devaki Ke Balram.
Krishna Chalisa Padhat Hi, Puran Hot Sab Kam.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Shri Krishna Dayanidhi Data.
Jag Ke Palanhar Bidhata.
Devaki Tum Maiya Ke Udhav.
Yashoda Ke Hit Ki Parchhain.
Nand Ke Anand Badhavan.
Gokul Mein Anand Machavan.
Braj Mein Dhoom Machavan Leela.
Sur-Nar-Muni Man Harne Khela.
Maiya Ke Sang Makhan Bhavai.
Chori Kar Navneet Udavai.
Gwalan Sang Gaiyya Charavai.
Bansi Ki Dhun Sab Man Bhavai.
Gopiyan Ke Sang Ras Rachavai.
Murali Ki Madhur Dhun Gavai.
Kalindi Mein Kaliya Natha.
Chape Vishdhar Liye Satha.
Govardhan Giri Ko Uthaya.
Indra Ka Maan Khoob Ghataya.
Mathura Mein Kans Vadh Keena.
Ugrasen Ko Raj Pad Deena.
Guru Sandipan Se Vidya Pai.
Mitra Sudama Ki Sudhi Aai.
Dwarika Nagri Basai.
Rukmini Sang Vivah Rachai.
Pandavan Ke Sakha Kahaye.
Arjun Ke Sarthi Ban Aaye.
Vishwaroop Arjun Dikhaya.
Geeta Gyan Jagat Ko Daya.
Dharm Ki Sada Raksha Keeni.
Dushtan Ko Sab Saja Deeni.
Draupadi Ki Laj Bachai.
Ant Viheen Cheer Badhai.
Bhishm Pran Ki Rakhi Laja.
Dharmraj Ko Dinho Raja.
Solah Kala Sampoorn Avatara.
Karm-Dharm Ka Deep Ujara.
Jarasandh Ko Dinho Mari.
Shishupal Vadh Ki Taiyari.
Narkasur Ka Nash Karaya.
Solah Sahas Kanya Ko Laya.
Gopi Gopan Ko Sukh Deena.
Bhaktan Par Sada Kripa Keena.
Meera Thi Deewani Tori.
Surdas Ki Aankh Na Mori.
Narsi Ka Kasht Nivara.
Bhaktan Ko Sada Pyara.
Mor Mukut Pitambar Dhari.
Kundal Shravan Manohar Bhari.
Vaijayanti Maal Viraje.
Naynan Mein Anjan Saje.
Murali Adhar Madhur Muskan.
Sakal Srishti Ke Tum Bhagwan.
Krishna Chalisa Jo Gavai.
Baikunth Parampad Pavai.
Jo Koi Shuddh Bhav Se Padhai.
Tan-Man-Dhan Ki Vipada Katai.

|| Doha ||
Shri Krishna Chalisa Nit, Padhai Jo Koi Dham.
Tako Sab Sukh Sampada, Milai Krishna Aviram.
hindiMeaning:
हाथ में सुंदर बांसुरी शोभा पा रही है, नीलमणि के समान श्याम शरीर है, चंद्रिका के समान मंद मुस्कान है - ऐसे नंदकिशोर को मेरा प्रणाम। जिन्होंने अर्जुन को गीता का ज्ञान दिया, जो देवकी के पुत्र हैं - उनकी कृष्ण चालीसा पढ़ने मात्र से सभी कार्य सिद्ध होते हैं।

हे श्रीकृष्ण! आपकी जय हो, आप दया के भंडार और दाता हैं। आप जगत के पालनहार और विधाता हैं। आप देवकी और यशोदा दोनों माताओं के प्रिय पुत्र हैं। नंद बाबा के आनंद बढ़ाने वाले, गोकुल में आनंद मचाने वाले हैं।
benefits: Invokes divine blessings of lord krishna
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles and sorrows from life
Invokes the blessing of peace, prosperity, and happiness
Helps attain spiritual growth and devotion
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of righteous desires when recited with faith
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Krishna Chalisa (कृष्ण चालीसा) is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn dedicated to Lord Krishna. Rooted in Traditional, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to remove all obstacles and sorrows from life and protect devotees from negative influences. It is also said to grant divine blessings of Lord Krishna. This prayer is traditionally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami to invoke divine grace. At 42 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 15 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Krishna Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Krishna Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration. Forty verses dedicated to Lord Krishna for blessings, peace and spiritual growth.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Krishna Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The Krishna Chalisa is traditionally attributed to Sant Sundardas (also spelt Sundar Das), a 17th-century Krishna-bhakti poet who wrote in vernacular Hindi to make Krishna devotion accessible to ordinary householders. The closing verses of the chalisa explicitly mention Sundardas asking Krishna to remove his ignorance, forgive his failings, and grant darshan — a typical bhanita (signature line) of medieval Hindi devotional poetry. While some printed editions list slightly different authorial details, Sundardas remains the most widely cited composer in Gita Press and other traditional publications. The text reflects the broader Bhakti movement's emphasis on a personal, loving relationship with Krishna over Sanskrit ritualism. (Sources: Drik Panchang; Hindu Gallery)

seoFaqs.question: What episodes from Krishna's life does the Chalisa highlight?
seoFaqs.answer: The Krishna Chalisa moves chronologically through the most beloved episodes of Krishna's lila. It mentions his birth in Mathura's prison to Devaki and Vasudeva, his transfer to Gokul where he was raised by Yashoda and Nanda, his childhood butter-stealing (makhan-chori), the killing of demons such as Putana and Kaliya, the lifting of Govardhan Hill to shelter the Brijwasis from Indra, the raas leela with the gopis, the marriage to Rukmini, his role as Arjuna's charioteer at Kurukshetra, and the delivery of the Bhagavad Gita. The chalisa thus serves as a compact poetic summary of the Bhagavata Purana's tenth canto. (Source: Durlabh Darshan; Lifeved)

seoFaqs.question: Why are Wednesday and Janmashtami the best days to recite Krishna Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Wednesday (Budhvar) is traditionally considered Krishna's day of the week in many regional traditions, though Thursday is also observed in some lineages. The most powerful day, however, is Janmashtami — the lunar birthday of Krishna falling on the Ashtami of Bhadrapada Krishna Paksha (August-September). Devotees observe a day-long fast, recite the Krishna Chalisa multiple times (often 11, 21, or 108), perform the midnight Janmotsav abhishek of the laddu-gopal murti, and break the fast after the abhishek. Ekadashi tithis, Radhashtami, and the Kartik month are also considered especially auspicious for chalisa recitation. (Source: Durlabh Darshan)

seoFaqs.question: What does the Krishna Chalisa say about siddhi and nidhi?
seoFaqs.answer: A celebrated verse of the Krishna Chalisa promises that the sincere reciter will obtain the ashta-siddhi (eight yogic powers) and nava-nidhi (nine treasures of Kubera). This phala-shruti is a direct echo of the Hanuman Chalisa's similar promise — both works share the chalisa form of Tulsidas-era Hindi devotional literature. In the Krishna Chalisa context, siddhi and nidhi are understood not merely as material wealth but as the spiritual fulfilment that comes from steady Krishna-bhakti: discriminative wisdom, contentment, fearlessness, and the company of devotees. The verse is often cited as the basis for performing 40-day or 108-day chalisa anushthans during Purushottam Maas. (Source: ServDharm Krishna Chalisa for Siddhi and Nidhi)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Krishna Chalisa different from the Bhagavad Gita?
seoFaqs.answer: The Bhagavad Gita is a 700-verse Sanskrit philosophical dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, addressing dharma, karma yoga, jnana yoga, and bhakti yoga. The Krishna Chalisa, by contrast, is a 40-chaupai vernacular Hindi devotional hymn that praises Krishna's beauty, lila, and saving grace. The Gita is a scripture of teaching; the Chalisa is a hymn of praise. Many traditional households use the two together — reading a Gita chapter daily for jnana and reciting the Krishna Chalisa for bhakti. The Chalisa briefly references Krishna's role as Arjuna's guide and the giver of the Gita, but its tone is loving and personal rather than philosophical. (Source: JKYog Bhagavad Gita Quotes)
